question.text: Speculative losses from commodity derivatives can be:

answer.text: Set off only against speculative profits — and carried forward for up to 4 years
Set off against any income including salary
Cannot be carried forward at all

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Speculative losses can only be set off against speculative profits (not other business or capital income). They can be carried forward for 4 assessment years. **The Trap:** Cannot set off against salary, capital gains, or non-speculative business income. Can be carried forward (but limited to 4 years). **Cross-Exam Context:** Loss set-off rules are important for tax planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 4-year carry forward for speculative losses is shorter than 8 years for business losses — another reason to seek hedging classification when genuinely hedging.
